1990_08_21_India-Mumbai_Thy_will_be_done_SB_8_6_38_st2_vr2

Event Name/Title

Thy will be done

Event Date

21 Aug 1990

Event Location

India, Mumbai

Event Type

Conference

Transcriber

Barbara Ferro

Completed

17 Sep 2021

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1990/90-066-Thy%20Will%20Be%20Done%20(Mumbai)-SB_8_6_39-dt_90_08_21.mp3

Source Video

Verse

SB 8.6.38, Bg. 3.27, Bg. 9.10, Śrī brahma-saṁhitā 5.1, Bg. 9.13, Bg. 18.66, Bg. 7.14, Bg. 9.22, Bg. 4.11, Bg. 9.34, CC Madhya 13.80, Bg. 4.34

Tags – Places

Govardhan hill, Vaikuntha, Goloka

Tags – Pastimes

Lord Brahma, Lord Krishna, Lord Narayan, Garuda, Srila Prabhupada, Bhaktivinoda Thakur

Tags – Persons

Prahlad, Arjuna

Tags – Themes

Surrender to Krishna, Guru, approching Krishna, devotional service

Om Namoh Bhagavate Vasudevaya

Giriṁ cāropya garuḍe

hastenaikena līlayā

āruhya prayayāv abdhiṁ

surāsura-gaṇair vṛtaḥ

[SB 8.6.38]

[The Lord very easily lifted the mountain with one hand and placed it on the back of Garuḍa. Then, He too got on the back of Garuḍa and went to the Ocean of Milk, surrounded by the demigods and demons.]

Purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedantha Prabhupada

Here is proof of the omnipotence of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is above everyone. There are two classes of living entities — the demons and the demigods — and the Supreme Personality of Godhead is above them both. The demons believe in the “chance” theory of creation, whereas the demigods believe in creation by the hand of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. The omnipotence of the Supreme Lord is proved here, for simply with one hand He lifted Mandara Mountain, the demigods and the demons, placed them on the back of Garuḍa and brought them to the Ocean of Milk. Now, the demigods, the devotees, would immediately accept this incident, knowing that the Lord can lift anything, however heavy it might be. But although demons were also carried along with the demigods, demons, upon hearing of this incident, would say that it is mythological. But if God is all-powerful, why would it be difficult for Him to lift a mountain? Since He is floating innumerable planets with many hundreds and thousands of Mandara Mountains, why can’t He lift one of them with His hand? This is not mythology, but the difference between the believers and the faithless is that the devotees accept the incidents mentioned in the Vedic literatures to be true, whereas the demons simply argue and label all these historical incidents mythology. Demons would prefer to explain that everything happening in the cosmic manifestation takes place by chance, but demigods, or devotees, never consider anything to be chance. Rather, they know that everything is an arrangement of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. That is the difference between the demigods and the demons.

Translation, the Lord very easily lifted the mountain with one hand and placed it on the back of Garuḍa. Then, He too got on the back of Garuḍa and went to the Ocean of Milk, surrounded by the demigods and demons.

Lecture transcriptions

With these verses we have read of the great struggle that the Demi-Gods and the demons jointly undertook in the attempt to move this enormous golden mountain of the name Mandara. We have read that many of them were great maharathis, powerful warriors, controllers of huge inconceivable functions of the entire universe. This mountain of gold was such a challenge for them that, in the process of trying to move it, they were straining and struggling with all of their energy. They were screaming and howling in pain because it was so difficult and despite all this the mountain kept falling and crushing their limbs and even crushing their bodies to death. And here are the most powerful rulers of the entire universe. At that time Lord Narayan, on his hand-bird carrier Garuda and effortlessly with one hand, he lifted the mountain and put it on the back of Garuda. This is the difference between God and even the most powerful of all living beings. Whoever we are, we are insignificant before Krishna.

What is our position compared to Lord Brahma?

In the Brahma Vimohan Lila, in the Bhagavat Purana, we read of the most powerful of all the devatas, Lord Brahma and how after seeing the inconceivable opulence of Sri Krishna, Lord Brahma was comparing himself to be a firefly, who in the night appears to be generating great quantities of light, but in the presence of the sun the firefly has utterly no significance whatsoever. Now what is our position compared to Lord Brahma? Lord Brahma lives for trillions and trillions of years. Lord Brahma is the creator of everything that we see in this entire universe. He is the direct agent of Krishna for the purpose of creation. Sometimes we create something we think, ‘We are very good’. We decorate the temple nicely in this tiny little room, in this tiny little city, we think, ‘Aah! Just see what we have done’. We build some factory somewhere, we think, ‘Oh! I have built this factory’ huh. We have a nice home, we think, ‘Aah! I am single handedly supporting this nice home’.

Lord Brahma has created all planets and everything within all planets and yet when he came before Krishna he understood, prakṛteḥ kriyamāṇāni guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśaḥ ahaṅkāra-vimūḍhātmā kartāham iti manyate [Bg. 3.27]. The bewildered spirit soul thinks himself to be the doer of activities. Actually they are being carried out by nature. Mayādhyakṣeṇa prakṛtiḥ sūyate sa-carācaram hetunānena kaunteya jagad viparivartate [Bg. 9.10] and when we speak of nature we must understand that this material nature is working under the direction of Krishna. It is his nature. Therefore, he is sarva-kāraṇa-kāraṇam [Śrī brahma-saṁhitā 5.1], he is the cause of all causes, he is the ultimate doer of everything. Nothing happens by chance. Everything is taking place by the divine direction of Sri Hari.

Therefore, this idea of accident, oh, is a word that is used very loosely, maybe by our calculations as far as according to our plans, something maybe an accident. But on a higher level there is no accidents. For devotee everything is being orchestrated by Krishna’s divine hand and whatever good or bad is taking place it is all for the ultimate good of that devotee, although sometimes we cannot understand how or why. Krishna puts us in certain situations which appear to be detrimental, but nothing is detrimental for a devotee who has faith in God. Everything is a perfect opportunity for spiritual development, for spiritual advancement. And for the karmis, aah! They are under the direction of the laws of karma. The actions and reactions of material nature are constantly coming upon them. But as Krishna explains, this is all happening under his direction. He has created the laws of karma. Therefore, whoever we are in all 8,400,000 species of life, at every second, at every moment of our existence, nothing is happening by chance, everything is taking place according to the divine will of the Lord.

Being a mahatma means to keep himself under the direction of the divine energy

Mahātmānas tu māṁ pārtha

daivīṁ prakṛtim āśritāḥ

bhajanty ananya-manaso

jṣātvā bhūtādim avyayam

[Bg. 9.13]

[O son of Pṛthā, those who are not deluded, the great souls, are under the protection of the divine nature. They are fully engaged in devotional service because they know Me as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, original and inexhaustible.]

This means that the difference between a devotee and a demon or a non-devotee is that a devotee puts himself under the direction of the spiritual energy, daivi prakriti and the non-devotee puts himself under the maya prakriti or the illusory energy. Now what is the difference between daivi prakriti and maya prakriti? It is the difference between our own mental concoction and accepting the higher authority of the will of God. That is the only difference. If we act according to our own conclusions, according to our own conceptions then we are putting ourselves under the influence of maya shakti. But it says, if we surrender and accept submissively the supreme will of God, which is coming through Guru, Sadhu and Shastra, then immediately we are under the daivi prakriti, this spiritual energy. Such a person, Krishna declares to be a mahatma, a great soul. Being a mahatma doesn’t mean being a great speaker. Being a mahatma doesn’t mean being a great pujari, it doesn’t even mean being a sannyasi or a guru. Being a mahatma means daivīṁ prakṛtim āśritāḥ, one who keeps himself under the direction of the divine energy. That means one who submits himself before God. That is a mahatma and that is the very essence of Krishna consciousness. To put aside our own conclusions and accept the will of God. Then Krishna explains that such a person is full under his protection. Such a person has nothing to fear. But as soon as we act according to our own whims, we have everything to fear because our life is in our own hands. So therefore, this is the great vision of the maha bhagavats. They see that Krishna is the controller and he is the protector and he is always the up lifter of those who surrender to his lotus feet. They have this faith. They have this confidence that, “Krishna if I simply – Thy will be done not my will be done”.

If we accept this conclusion, then Krishna explains in Gita, sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ [Bg. 18.66]. Notice how he explains, very important. Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall protect you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear. He ends the most essential and critical verse of Bhagavad-gita with these words, ‘Do not fear’. What does that mean? In this material world, as long as we put our life in our own hands, according to our own conclusions, we have everything to fear, because everything will be taken away from us. We will be embarrassed by the laws of material nature inevitably. There is nothing you can do about it. But if you surrender to Krishna, you have nothing to fear. Why? Because you are in the hands of God. This is his promise. This is his verdict. Sometimes when someone comes to Krishna consciousness, we think how will things happen, how will I be maintained? Lord Jesus Christ, when his followers, he told them, “Follow me” and they said, “What about our families, what about our occupation, what about our clothing and what about our food?” And he said, “Don’t even think about these things, don’t even consider these things, have faith. If the Lord is providing for the birds and the bees and the insects and every blade of grass and they are just inanimate living entities who have no consciousness of God whatsoever. And he is also maintaining the demons, who are against God. You do not think that the same loving father who is within your heart, he will not provide what you need if you surrender your life to his will? What do you have to fear?” We are not concerned with the externals, we are concerned with the spirit of bhakti, which means to accept the will of God.

The sins within our heart has created an immovable mountain

So therefore, in this beautiful chapter of Srimad-bhagavatam, it is so wonderfully explained through the example of the Lord himself, how when Krishna takes upon himself to help you, what doubt or questions did you have in regard to whether everything is alright? The Demigods and the demons, they could not lift this mountain for more than a few seconds at a time. What to speak of carrying it for miles and miles and miles away? They had to carry it not only miles, they had to carry it to another planet, where the ocean of milk was. It was an impossible feat, even for the greatest rulers of the world. But Krishna wanted to show, not only them, but he wanted to show all of us, that if he wants to lift something, there is nothing too heavy for him to lift. He simply lifted the mountain effortlessly.

Sometimes the sins within our heart are compared to a mountain. The accumulation of sin after sin after sin for millions of births, has created an immovable mountain. And sometimes when we try to become free from our material propensities, it seems impossible. How many of you have had that experience? The conditionings towards the attraction of material life even though we are trying and struggling so hard, whether brahmacharis, grihastas, vanaprasthas or sannyasis, is very difficult. This material energy, Krishna says, “daivī hy eṣā guṇa-mayī mama māyā duratyayā mām eva ye prapadyante māyām etāṁ taranti te” [Bg. 7.14]. It’s impossible to overcome. It’s like you single handedly trying to move one of the Himalayan mountains with your own hands. That’s the chances you have of giving up all your material inclinations in life. So this mountain of material attraction of sinful desire, sinful means any desire apart from the will of Guru and Krishna, it is sinful. This mountain is within our heart and it seems immovable, impossible. How to move it? It’s not a question of whether in this position or that position, it’s a question of unless Krishna comes and personally moves it for us, you are helpless. But when Krishna sees that we are sincere, when Krishna sees that we are obediently trying to assist his divine plan, when he sees that we are willing to make his will our will, in other words when we surrender, then Krishna takes this mountain right from our heart. He takes our soul and lifts it up.

This story although it is facts and history, it is also very symbolic. A very, very inner truth. How Krishna can effortlessly lift this mountain is the same way he can effortlessly lift the mountain of sins and material attractions that are within your heart. If you simply like the Demigods and the demons, they accepted his will. Understand the demons, that see what people they are, but because they accepted Krishna’s will, he lifted this mountain for them. It was Krishna’s will that the Demigods and the demons work together for the sake of moving this mountain. And even though it is an impossible feat because they simply accepted, yes, we will try to do it. This is the will of God. Even though the demons had so many ulterior motives, because they accepted the will of God, Krishna took it upon himself.

Ananyāś cintayanto māṁ ye janāḥ paryupāsate teṣāṁ nityābhiyuktānāṁ yoga-kṣemaṁ vahāmy aham [Bg. 9.22]. Krishna preserves that he will carry what we lack but not until we give all of our energy. It’s not that he will carry what you lack if your just a lazy fellow thinking Krishna will do it. First you have to try as hard as you can. If you try as hard as you can whatever deficiencies you have, Krishna will come and effortlessly take care. You see, Krishna did not even consider lifting this mountain, until the demons and the Demigods, put all of their effort into it. And when they put their lives and souls into this project of moving this mountain, then Krishna said, “Alright you have given everything that you have, whatever is the balance, I will take”. This is how Krishna deals with his devotees. If you give everything you have to serve Guru and serve Krishna, then whatever the balances for the purpose of executing that order, Krishna will accept. He will accept that responsibility, that is his promise. Therefore, we have nothing to fear. At any level a devotee is abhaya-caraṇāravinda re, he is fearless because he knows that when you surrender to Krishna, when you surrender to Guru’s will, then you’re in the hands of God. Why should I think may this, maybe that, but what about this and what about that. This is the material world of duality. When you surrender to Krishna you are no longer under the laws of material duality. You’re in Krishna’s hands. He takes abhaya-caraṇāravinda re, nothing to fear. Of course, as Srila Prabhupada is explaining in this purport that the demoniac class of men, they are not willing to accept the intricacies of the potencies of the Lord. Therefore, when they read such stories, as the story from Srimad-bhagavatam, they consider that it is mythology, they consider that it is fiction. It’s not true. It represents some very philosophical um idea which is very important for the common man to understand but there’s no historical truth behind these stories. After all how can anyone lift a mountain? How can anyone marry 16108 wives and have a palace for each one of them? How can anyone of them open their mouth and show the whole universe within it? They are seeing the gross atheists, they are seeing that it is all just fiction, it is the opiate of the masses, it is just to intoxicate people and make people counter-productive to real progress in society, all these stories. Therefore, they should be abandoned. People who are little more pious, they say, “Oh! Well the message behind these stories is very good, because the common man is basically ignorant you know they are not so highly educated and sophisticated in their philosophical prowess as us, so therefore they require these simple little stories to teach them some lessons, but there is no truth to these stories”.

What is surrender?

Why do they think like this? For one reason alone, because they do not want to surrender. Because as soon as you accept God is a person, that God does exist then he is the Supreme Personality of Godhead, then you have to accept that he can do anything. And if he is a person and if he can do anything, he is not just a fictional character, that he is a reality, then you have to surrender to him. You have to obey him. They do not want to surrender, they do not want to obey. They want to live according to their perceptions of what they like and what they do not like. But surrender means no more considering what I like or what I don’t like. Surrender means no more considering what I think is good or what I think is bad. Surrendering even means not even considering my conceptions towards spiritual advancement. Surrendering means,

tad viddhi praṇipātena

paripraśnena sevayā

upadekṣyanti te jṣānaṁ

jṣāninas tattva-darśinaḥ

[Bg. 4.34]

[Just try to learn the truth by approaching a spiritual master. Inquire from him submissively and render service unto him. The self-realized souls can impart knowledge unto you because they have seen the truth.]

Krishna explains what is surrender. If you want to know the truth, you approach Guru. You inquire submissively. What does it mean to enquire submissively? How can I serve. That’s what it means. And in this service you enquire how can I serve and then you do it. Very simple. Krishna consciousness is simple for the simple and complicated for the complicated. What does it mean to be complicated? To think that I know anything. As long as you think that you know something, your whole life is complicated, because you will always be acting according to your minds command. Simple means, I do not know anything. I don’t know what it means to be a devotee. I don’t know what it means to make spiritual advancement. I don’t know what it is God. I don’t know anything. Therefore, Guru Maharaj what can I do, how can I serve? That is Bhagavad-gita, that is Srimad-bhagavatam, that is bhakti. And you see, if you accept Krishna as the almighty personality, in the reality, not only historical reality but in eternal reality and you are face to face with him 24 hours a day then you have to submit to yourself, you have to surrender. You have to accept Guru and you have to surrender because that’s God’s order. But men they do not want to accept the Guru. They don’t want to accept God. They want to act according to their own desires. Therefore, to cover up their egocentric demoniac way of viewing this world, they have to somehow or the other minimize the existence of God by calling his divine lila mythology.

The spiritual master knows exactly how to deal with everybody, to establish the truth

I remember one time Srila Prabhupada was talking with a very, very learned professor of Hindology. Hindology means a professor of the studies of the philosophy and the history and the religion of India. A very, very learned man by material standards and he explained to Prabhupad that these stories of the Vedas, he said, “They are very good, very, very good message but they are mythology. They are not real stories. Who can do like this? Who could lift the Govardhan hill with a little finger of his left hand? Be serious”. Srila Prabhupada said, “You are a rascal. You are a teacher, you are a professor, you are saying that you know and you are teaching the people, the young people like this. This is your concoction. You have no right to teach, unless you are teaching the truth and you have no access to the truth, unless you accept a Guru. You cannot interpret the Vedas by your own mental speculation. You have to accept a Guru. You have to enquire submissively. You have to render service. Otherwise, you are not entitled to teach Vedas”. So, of course this man was not very happy about this, because he was a very wealthy fellow, because he was famous author, famous professor in a great university. But Prabhupad was not concerned, not only that but several of his students were spreading rumors. Prabhupad said, “You are a rascal”. In fact, Prabhupad told his secretary, “Take him out of the room. I don’t want to see his face. He’s cheating, he’s cheating with his students”. See those different levels of inter-faith preaching [Maharaj laughs]. Sometimes, according to the time and circumstances, spiritual master knows exactly how to deal with everybody, to establish the truth. And Bhaktivinoda Thakur revealed to Srila Prabhupad sometimes, when he would preach to people, they would say, “He would be preaching about how you are not the body, but you are the soul. He’d be preaching to an atheist and they’d start arguing on the basis of trying to defeat points of the Bhagavad-gita. Prabhupad would say, “Forget Bhagavad-gita, let’s just talk logic”. Many times he would do this, “Forget Bhagavad-gita, forget all the scriptures let’s just talk logic”. And at other times, when someone would try to talk logic, he would just establish, “If you do not accept the word of God all your logic will never give you the conclusion of the truth”. And he would start reciting Bhagavad-gita. So the Guru, he is simply on behalf of Krishna, establishing the truth. And sometimes it appears like he is even contradicting himself. It even appears there are contradictions amongst the great acharyas. But there is no contradiction. 1, 2+2 is 4. 3+1 is 4. It appears like a contradiction. Two different ways of reaching 4. If you have a small mind, it is a contradiction. How can 3+1 is 4, because I already know that 2+ 2 is 4. How can he say that something else equals 4, ha. But if you have a higher understanding of mathematics, you know that there are no contradictions. Similarly, there are various sampradayas and they are approaching the absolute truth. Some are approaching from 3+1 some are approaching from 2+2, but they are all coming to the point of Krishna. They know the different realizations of Krishna, different levels of realization of Krishna, but they are all approaching Krishna. Therefore, the great Acharyas and the Lord himself in the various scriptures of the world, they are according to the kala, desh, patra, the time, place and person, they are teaching us how we can approach Krishna. Only if you follow those who are teaching devotion to Radha and Krishna, will you attain the supreme planet of Goloka Vrindavan. If you follow what the teachings of those who are presenting God as the supreme father only, then you will reach the level of Vaikunta. There’s a different lila in Vaikunta than Goloka. If you are approaching those who are teaching piety, then at the most you will attain the heavenly planets. So different Gurus, different saintly persons and different sampradayas, they are all teaching different aspects of the one supreme God.

But God is infinite Bhagavan and what and how we approach him, he reveals himself accordingly, ye yathā māṁ prapadyante tāṁs tathaiva bhajāmy aham [Bg. 4.11]. As you surrender to Krishna he reveals himself accordingly. But if you do not surrender, he will not reveal himself personally. He will simply reveal himself through material nature. It’s not a question of whether I’m a devotee or not. Sometimes we think, ‘Ah! I’m a devotee’. So therefore, I have nothing to fear. Devotee means to surrender. Surrendering means not my will but thy will be done. Krishna says in Gita, Man-manā bhava mad-bhakto [Bg. 9.34], become my devotee, always think of me, worship me. But how to become his devotee? By surrendering.

Sarva-dharmān parityajya [Bg. 18.66], by surrendering. But what do we know about surrendering. We talk about surrendering, we know nothing about surrendering. Krishna tests us. Surrendering means, you have to put aside all your conceptions of what it means to make spiritual advancement. You have to put all the conceptions away, of what it means to be happy in this world and you have to simply accept, as Arjuna accepted. Arjuna was asked by Krishna to do what he hated to do more than anything on earth. He would’ve rather become a sweeper of the street than kill the people he loved huh. Surrender meant that Arjuna had to put aside his pride, he had to put aside his ego. He had to put aside his own conditional conceptions, what is good, what is bad, what is beautiful, what is material and he simply had to accept, yes. Thy will not my will be done. But you want Krishna, that is my life and soul. And of course, we are not so advanced that Krishna is telling us directly. Therefore, Krishna says himself, [34.08 inaudible] ata vijṣāna tams guruneva. If you want to understand Krishna you must approach Guru, you must approach sadhu. They will explain the real essence of the shastras. And through this process, by being humble servant, material nature cannot defeat us, because we are in Krishna’s hands. Krishna is not concerned with anything but our humble service attitude. That is the conclusion of all the teachings of Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

nāhaṁ vipro na ca nara-patir nāpi vaiśyo na śūdro

nāhaṁ varṇī na ca gṛha-patir no vanastho yatir vā

kintu prodyan-nikhila-paramānanda-pūrnāmṛtābdher

gopī-bhartuḥ pada-kamalayor dāsa-dāsānudāsaḥ

[Cc.Madhya 13.80]

[I am not a brāhmaṇa, I am not a kṣatriya , I am not a vaiśya or a śūdra. Nor am I a brahmacārī, a householder, a

vānaprastha or a sannyāsī. I identify Myself only as the servant of the servant of the servant of the lotus feet of

Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the maintainer of the gopīs. He is like an ocean of nectar, and He is the cause of universal

transcendental bliss. He is always existing with brilliance.]

Varnashram dharma. I am not a brahman, kshatriya, vaishya or shudra. I am not a brahmachari, grihasta, vanaprastha, sanyas. These are all dharma.

To be the servant of the servant of the Lord

Sarva-dharmān parityajya [Bg. 18.66], abandon all dharma. What does that mean? Gopī-bhartuḥ pada-kamalayor dāsa-dāsānudāsaḥ [CC Madhya 13.80] that I am only the servant of the servant of the servant of the servant of the Lord of the gopis, Sri Krishna.

Krishna consciousness means to become the servant of the servant of the servant. What does it mean to be servant? My will is not important. Thy will is important. I have faith in you. Arjuna had complete confidence that, “I will kill my relatives and although it appears that I am being very cruel, I have faith that it is all good because it is the direction of Krishna, ha”. There was a large leap of faith. But because he heard the words of God, he had that faith and that faith is most important, because based on that faith we have the courage to surrender. Surrender means Krishna, Prahlad, when he was thrown of a huge mountain, he was falling through the air, he was furious, sarva-dharmān parityajya [Bg. 18.66], he surrendered to Krishna fearless. He knew. He was surrendered. All he had to do was just compromise with his father and say, “Yes, yes, I’m not a devotee anymore”. His father would not, but he knew it was God’s will. God had a plan for him to show the world how he protects his devotee. So Prahlad accepted the position of challenging his own father and when he was thrown off that mountain, he was not going, “Aaahhh!!” He was just smiling, “Hare Krishna Hare Krishna”. Krishna will save you, of course he will save you. What’s the question whether he will save you or not? If you were falling from a mountain would you be thinking, ‘Oh my God I wonder if Krishna’s gonna save me. I’m almost just alive, only a few more seconds and I’ll hit the ground’. Picture, put yourself in Prahlad’s position. There’s a huge mountain, there’s nothing else between you and the ground except miles and miles of air, ha and you’re falling full speed down would you question or doubt what would be your fate, ha. Prahlad did not. Prahlad was fully confident that Krishna would protect him, because it’s his promise. I surrendered. Of course, he will protect me. That is the position of one who surrenders and when you surrender to Krishna never consider yourself to be the loser. You are being hundreds and millions of times, there’s no loss ever for one who surrenders to Krishna. Because when you surrender to Krishna you are given a position in his own heart and when you have a position in Krishna’s heart, you can know that 24 hours a day at all times Krishna will be there to guide you, to protect you, to lead you on, so what do you have to fear? He will come, even in the most struggling moments of life and he will lift the burden of the mountain of all of our sinful karma. He will lift the burden of the mountain of all of the problems of the material existence.

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
